findings.title: Figure 4 MSE-rho curve computed on training data, paper says test set
findings.quote:
model = LinearRegression()
model.fit(pred_train.reshape((-1, n)), noise_train.reshape((-1, n)))
reg_pred = model.predict(pred_train.reshape((-1, n)).reshape((-1, n)))
metric_record = []

for i in range(n):
    mse = mean_squared_error(noise_train.reshape((-1, n))[:, i], reg_pred[:, i])
    r2 = r2_score(noise_train.reshape((-1, n))[:, i], reg_pred[:, i])
    metric_record.append([mse, r2])
findings.claim: The MSE/R^2 plotted in Figure 4 are computed by predicting on pred_train and comparing to noise_train, i.e. on the training split, while pred_test/noise_test are used only for the control-cost simulation.
findings.concern: Appendix A.5.1 states the MSE-rho curve is plotted on a 16000-sample held-out test set; the code reports in-sample (training) MSE instead, a code-vs-paper mismatch.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm whether Figure 4 should report test-set MSE (predict on pred_test vs noise_test); with 64000 training samples the two values differ only at ~1e-3 (see _audit_code/out/example33.txt), so the figure and the equal-MSE conclusion are unaffected.

findings.title: M-GAPS decaying learning rate in code differs from Appendix A.6 formula
findings.quote:
# use a decay learning rate
lr = 1e-3 / np.sqrt(1 + i / 100)
pol_opt.policy_update(lr=lr)
findings.claim: Both policy-optimization scripts use the step-size 1e-3 * (1 + t/100)^{-0.5} (same line in Example_Policy_Optimization_2.py:70).
findings.concern: Appendix A.6 states the decaying learning rate sequence is eta_t = (1 + t/1000)^{-0.5}; the code uses a different time constant (100 vs 1000) and an explicit 1e-3 prefactor not shown in the paper formula.
findings.resolution: Authors: reconcile the learning-rate schedule used to generate Figures 1-2 with the formula reported in Appendix A.6 (the code's schedule is itself a valid decaying step size, so the convergence illustration stands).
findings.paper_ref: Appendix A.6 ('decaying learning rate sequence eta_t = (1 + t/1000)^{-0.5}')
